titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Hyla S. Watters Papers
titlestmt.author: Abigail Kromminga

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid prepared according to Describing Archives: A Content Standard (DACS).

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday November 7, 2022 at 23:00

did.abstract: This collection documents the life and work of medical missionary Hyla Stowell Watters (1893-1987). Watters served at Wuhu General Hospital in Anhui Province, China, and then at the Methodist Mission hospital in Ganta, Liberia, before spending her later years in Tupper Lake, New York. The collection also contains materials gathered by author Elsie Hayes Landstrom for the publication of Hyla Doc, two books based on Hyla's life.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Hyla S. Watters Papers (RG 328), Special Collections, Yale Divinity School Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Hyla Stowell Watters, known to friends as "Hyla Doc," was born in 1893 to Ada Stowell Watters and the Methodist pastor Philip M. Watters. While regularly moving across New York state for Philip Watters' career, the family built at cottage in Tupper Lake where they summered annually. Throughout her life, Hyla Watters considered Tupper Lake, New York her enduring home. Hyla graduated from Smith College in 1915 with a degree in Philosophy. After a brief interim teaching in Atlanta, where her father served as the president of Gammon Theological Seminary, Hyla continued her education at Cornell University Medical School, graduating in 1921. Inspired by the example of Dr. Edward C. Perkins, a close family friend who served as a doctor in Jiujiang (Kiukiang), and an interest in Christian missions in China which began in childhood, Hyla undertook further medical training at the School of Tropical Medicine in London and sailed to China in 1924. Following a year of intensive language study at Nanking University, Hyla served from 1925-1941 at Wuhu General Hospital in Anhui Province, China. In 1941, when Wuhu was under the Japanese occupation, Hyla Watters wrote, "[we] are planning to stay on as long as possible. One remembers the verse from the wall-builders of Jerusalem, 'I am doing a great work, and cannot come down.'" Though interned by the Japanese and expatriated to the United States shortly after, Hyla traveled back to China in 1945 with the Red Cross and served in the reopened Wuhu General Hospital until 1948. After nearly a quarter century of living in Wuhu, prompted by the concerns of her colleagues amidst the advancement of communist forces, Hyla S. Watters left China. In 1950 Hyla announced her commission from the Methodist Board of Foreign Missions to go to Ganta, Liberia, stating, "I am looking forward to working in the Liberian hinterland, and to returning to China later when the way opens." From 1950 to 1961 Hyla worked aiding the local clinic's development into a hospital and lived with her companion and friend Mildred Black, who worked in language education. Upon reaching the age of required retirement for the Methodist Board in 1961, Hyla traveled across the continent of Africa with Mildred Black, en route to Tupper Lake. Reflecting on her years in Ganta relative to her time in China for the Adirondack Daily Enterprise Weekender in 1980, Hyla observed, "we never got closely involved with our co-workers there…in China we had wars and floods and pestilence. The adversity brought us together." From 1961 until her death in 1987, Hyla worked in Tupper Lake as a physician until the age of eighty-seven and spoke regularly at community events. In 1980, as the People's Republic of China opened to American visitors, Hyla travelled back to China with Mildred Black, visiting the hospital and colleagues from her years in Wuhu a half century before. In a 1973 interview, Hyla compared Christian missions¬¬—her life's labor—to a mountain she saw in Korea standing despite a hole borne through the rock, stating, "nothing that is as beautiful as that can ever be destroyed." In the final years of her life, Hyla spoke extensively with Elsie Hayes Landstrom, the child of missionaries in Wuhu, who compiled Hyla's memories from lifetime of medical and missionary work into two books published posthumously written in Hyla's voice: Hyla Doc in China and Hyla Doc in Africa . Further resources and information on Hyla S. Watters are available at Smith College Special Collections, in the Hyla S. Watters papers: Collection: Hyla S. Watters papers | Smith College Finding Aids. Also of interest may be the papers of Hyla's mentor Edward C. Perkins, which are held at Yale: Perkins, Edward C. (M.D.) Perkins, Georgina | Archives at Yale. Further materials on Elsie Hayes Landstrom, who edited the memoirs of Hyla S. Watters, are available in the Hayes Family Papers: Collection: Hayes Family Papers | Archives at Yale

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection documents the life and work of medical missionary Hyla Stowell Watters (1893-1987). Watters served at Wuhu General Hospital in Anhui Province, China, and then at the Methodist Mission hospital in Ganta, Liberia, before spending her later years in Tupper Lake, New York. The collection also contains materials gathered by author Elsie Hayes Landstrom for the publication of Hyla Doc, two books based on Hyla's life. Scope and Contents: The Hyla Watters Papers (RG 328) document the life of medical missionary Hyla Stowell Watters and contain the collected research for the posthumously published Hyla Doc books, edited by Elsie Hayes Landstrom. Substantive letters and writing from Hyla's years at the Wuhu General Hospital (1925-1941) and her return to China in 1945, provide insight into intercultural and interreligious relations among the greater Wuhu community, her work as a surgeon, her work with the United Nations Relief and Rehabilitation Administration (UNRRA) in the post World War II era, and the political environment in China from the 1920s until Communist rule. The material gathered by Elise Hayes Landstrom, which makes up the second part of this collection (Series VI), consists of transcribed letters and an extensive collection of taped interviews Landstrom conducted with Hyla Watters in her later years. Landstrom's material provides a nearly exhaustive archive of Hyla Watters' life—stretching from her early memories, through her work in China and Liberia, and her later years as an active community member and physician in Tupper Lake, New York. Series I contains the correspondence of Hyla S. Watters, spanning from her early years at the Nanking Language School in 1925 to letters received the year of her death in Tupper Lake, New York, 1987. Within the collection, Hyla's years at Wuhu General Hospital are well-documented in family circulars written by Hyla alone or jointly with her mother Ada Stowell Watters, who moved to China in 1926, and sister Florence (Sally) Watters Stuntz, a missionary in India. In the letters from 1945-1947, Hyla describes her return to China with the United States army, and journey once more to Wuhu, as well as her work with the UNRRA and CNRRA. Related photographs of UNRRA doctors and famine relief work are in Series V, Photographs. Correspondence dating from 1973-1987 is solely addressed to Hyla Watters from friends and colleagues. Notable within the later correspondence are the letters from Hyla's former student Enmei Yu, describing her experiences as a physician in the People's Republic of China. Further correspondence is found within Series VI subseries 1 "Compiled materials and drafts." To write the text of Hyla Doc, Elsie Hayes Landstrom initially gathered around 1,000 pages of transcribed letters. The resulting 1986 compilation reflects correspondence beyond the scope of Series I, likely provided by family members and the Hyla S. Watters papers at Smith College Special Collections Collection: Hyla S. Watters papers | Smith College Finding Aids. Series II, Writings and Notes consists of original writings by Hyla S. Watters, primarily vignettes she composed while serving at Wuhu Hospital as well as talk notes she wrote for speeches around Tupper Lake. The writings, dating from the 1920s into the 1930s, describe the Wuhu community, Hyla's medical work, and Christian themes. The talk notes, dating from 1964-1976, reflect Hyla's life within the Tupper Lake community, speaking at churches and local events on missions, scripture, and her philosophy of retirement. Series III, Collected Material, begins with a variety of pamphlets referencing Hyla's work in both Wuhu and Ganta. Collected publications and newspaper articles show Hyla's continuing interest in Chinese affairs. The series ends with miscellaneous material and Chinese cutout art gathered during Hyla's time in Wuhu. Series IV, Personal Items and Memorabilia, contains materials dating from 1892 to 1989. Materials in the initial folders provide insight into both family genealogy and the broad chronology of Hyla's life. The bulk of the material relates to Hyla's time in Tupper Lake from 1961 until her death in 1987. During this time, she was featured regularly in local newspaper articles. Series IV, subseries 2 contains watercolors painted by Hyla during her time in Liberia until the end of her life. The photographs in Series V, dating from 1895 to 1987, include both family photos and images related to Hyla's work in China and Africa. The Series begins with family portraits and images from Hyla's early life and proceeds roughly chronologically. Substantial photos date from Hyla's years in China, depicting Hyla and Ada in their Wuhu home, scenes from the hospital including the nursing staff and images of patients, and portraits of friends and colleagues. Multiple folders of proofs most likely date from Hyla's return to China and work with the UNRRA in 1945-1948. Photographs from Hyla's years in Liberia contain pictures with her housemate and friend Mildred Black, including images from their 1961 trip across the African continent. Later images consist primarily of portraits taken during the years Hyla lived in Tupper Lake. Series VI, Materials and Drafts Related to Hyla Doc Books, contains research materials and drafts related to the book Hyla Doc, which was published in two volumes. The first volume. Hyla Doc: Surgeon in China Through War and Revolution 1924-1949 (1991) documents Hyla's experience at Wuhu General Hospital and the second volume, Hyla Doc in Africa 1950-1961 (1994) documents her work at the Ganta Mission in Liberia. Although published posthumously, the books are written entirely in Hyla's voice. Elsie Hayes Landstrom compiled and edited extensive materials, gathering text for the later books through transcribing Hyla's correspondence and driving from her own home in Conway, Massachusetts to Hyla's home in Tupper Lake, New York. The resulting drafts, which provide a nearly comprehensive archive of Hyla's correspondence from her time at Smith until her later years in Tupper Lake, are held in box 3 of this collection. Landstrom described the community effort of the composition process in the introduction to Hyla Doc: Surgeon in China Through War and Revolution 1924-1949, "Shel Horowitz…undertook the enormous job of retyping these letters. Fortunately, to fill in gaps, Elizabeth Allen (Hyla Doc's niece) and her husband Daniel and I had taped many stories. But there remained pages of questions to ask Hyla Doc. Many remain mysteries, but some answers were found and spliced into her story from letters from her mother, Ada Stowell Watters, and from other members of the I-chi-san community." The ironing out of the text into its finished form is reflected in the multiple drafts contained in Series IV: the 1986 draft consists primarily of letters stitched together across stages in Hyla's life, by the 1988 draft, Landstrom smoothed out the transitions to appear as a single, continuous narration. Series VI also contains cassette tapes of interviews with Hyla, conducted between 1980 and 1987, many of which are transcribed in the 1986 compilation. The series ends with letters from Elsie Landstrom regarding the publication of the books, reviews, and the published monographs.

list.item: Materials and Drafts related to Publication of Hyla Doc,

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Place names were modernized in the description, with the name originally used in the collection material or in an older version of the finding aid in parenthesis: e.g. "Beijing (Peking)" or "Benin (Dahomey)".

did.unittitle: Including from: Hyla, Ada Stowell Watters, Florence Watters Stuntz

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Circulars and family letters from Hyla S. Watters at Nanking Language School, 1924-1925. Circulars and family letters from Hyla, Ada Stowell Watters, and Florence Watters Stuntz addressed from Wuhu, Anhui Province, China and Guling ("Kuling"), Jiujiang, China, 1929-1931.

did.unittitle: Including from: Hyla, Ada Stowell Watters, Florence Watters Stuntz, Clyde Allen, Paul Sommerfreund

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Circulars and family letters from Hyla S. Watters, Ada Stowell Watters, and Florence Watters Stuntz, addressed from Wuhu, Anhui, China. January 22, 1936 circular from Clyde Allen regarding his mission work in Lahore, West Pakistan with a handwritten note from Sally Watters Stuntz. January 5, 1939 transcribed letter from Dr. Paul Sommerfreund, a Jewish refugee and medical doctor from Vienna. Later circulars, 1939-1941, from Hyla describing work at Wuhu General Hospital in wartime.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters from Hyla S. Watters describing her return to China. Located in Chengdu ("Chengtu"), Sichuan Province, China, before traveling through Congquing ("Chungking") to Wuhu.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Circulars and family letters and personal correspondence from Hyla S. Watters detailing her work with the UNRRA amd CNRRA.

did.unittitle: Including from: Hyla, James K. Matthews

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Circulars and personal letters addressed from Hyla S. Watters at Ganta Mission, Ganta, Liberia. July 19, 1954 letter from James K. Matthews to Daniel D. Allen regarding the purchase of land at Mathura.

did.unittitle: Including from: Norman Vincent Peale, Enmei Yu, Wang Ping, Kay Davison, Isaac Shih

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Personal correspondence addressed to Hyla S. Watters, including letters from friends and former students in the People's Republic of China.

did.unittitle: Including from: Amicia Carroll, Enmei Yu, John Ting, Dora Lee

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Personal correspondence addressed to Hyla S. Watters. Continued correspondence from Enmei Yu, a former student of Hyla's, working at the Provincial Medical Information Institute in Sinchuan, China.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Christmas cards and messages sent from Wuhu General Hospital.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "The Christmas Play at Wuhu" 1926, "Devils and Toes" n.d. (1920s), "A Night Signal" 1931, "The Beautiful Glass Eye" n.d. (1930s)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "For My Mother I ate That Bitterness" 1935, "Dear Friends" 1935, "The Method About Sin" 1936

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Hurry Up and Laugh!" 1939, "A New Way to Eat." 1939, "How I Heard It" by George A. Hallett September 3, 1939, "How They Left." September 6, 1939, "The Polliwog Man" 1939, "Outstanding Ears" 1939, "East and West" n.d., "Making the Best of It" n.d. "Making the Best of It," copy with handwritten note from Jinny to Betty (Hyla Watters' niece).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Account of childhood titled "Growing-up Days." n.d., Easter play "As it Began to Dawn" n.d. "Why Do They Go?" News: Indiana Council of Churches n.d., "Out of the Bush" World Outlook n.d.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Prepared notes for talks at community and church events. Including reflections on various Christian scripture, and themes like, "Claimed by God for Mission," "Philosophy of Retirement."

did.unittitle: Talk notes and letters of appreciation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various reflections on Christian scripture. Materials related to the 1976 speech "Senior Citizens Philosophy of Retirement."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Program introducing the guest speaker Doctor Hyla Watters, n.d. Collected notes on the theme of freedom and liberty, Christian morality, various Christian biblical passages, and miscellaneous notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "A Second Visit to Kiukiang," 1913, by Edward C. Perkins. "Central China Conference Bulletin," Vol. 4 (no. 1), January 1937, issue devoted to Wuhu medical mission, including an image and brief article on Hyla Watters. "Ganta United Methodist Mission (1926-1976)," 1976, with forward by Bishop Bennie D. Warner.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Wuhu General Hospital Report to Conference," written by superintendent L.S. Morgan, 1940. "Bibliography on Posture," n.d.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Book given to Hyla Watters by Thomas Y., June 29, 1985.

did.unittitle: Yijishan Hospital of Wannan Medical College, booklet

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Yijishan Hospital of Wannan Medical College," booklet published 1989 commemorating the one-hundredth anniversary of the founding of the hospital.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "ITC's Dr. Harry Richardson: Success Of A Quiet Crusader," The Atlanta Journal and Constitution, March 17, 1979. "More Japanese Deny Nation Was Aggressor During World War II: Youths Learn Little of Era; Spread of Revisionist View Irks Many Other Asians; Was Nanking Really Raped?" The Wall Street Journal, September 8, 1988.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Wuhu black cutout pictures," of dragons, birds, ships, preserved between leaves of Wuhu General Hospital stationery. "Using a Traditional Craft in Christian Communications," handout compiled by China Talk, n.d.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Business card of Liu Zu Xin, director of Hefei Municipal Second People's Hospital, Hefei, Anhui Province. Stamps entitled, "China Life Saving Stamp." Card of membership written in Chinese to the Young Men's Christian Association (YMCA). Various photocopied documents written in Chinese, including one entitled "I stand by the side of a river."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sermon by Philip M. Watters, Hyla's father, and material from Philip Snedeker, Hyla's first cousin, and Judith Frissell, a distant relative. Extract of a letter from August 26, 1914 by Hyla's mentor and medical missionary in China, Edward C. Perkins. Biographical materials including business card, life chronology, and brief biography.

did.unittitle: Family compositions and gathered hymn texts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Texts to family compositions of songs and poetry, including from Hyla, Ada Stowell Watters, and Caroline A. Watters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written by Mildred Black who lived with Hyla in Liberia, which describes Hyla's work at the Ganta mission, a trip to Israel and Palestine, and a boat journey off the coast of Italy, Spain, arriving in Canada. Related images of Hyla and Mildred's trip across Africa in box 2, folder 27.

did.unittitle: Transcription of an interview with Hyla

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Interview, with Florence (Sally) Watters Stuntz, conducted by Jacqueline Van Voris for the Smith College Centennial Study on Hyla's career, Smith education, and Sally and Hyla's experience of global missions.

did.unittitle: New York State map, residency, and certification documents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Geology of Newcomb Quadrangle," map, n.d., University of the State of New York. Physician certification by the State Education Department, 1961, 1967, 1971, 1973. Certificate of Residence, 1977.

did.unittitle: Materials related to traveling back to China

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence from travel agencies and the Embassy of the People's Republic of China in Washington, D.C. regarding permission to travel to China. September 12, 1979 letter from Hyla Watters, petitioning for permission to visit.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Honors and awards, bulk from 1969-1981.

did.unittitle: Newspaper articles about Hyla S. Watters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Sorority Gives $615 For Scholarship," The St. Lawrence Plaindealer, December 18, 1974. "Retirement -- one more chance," Press-Republican, May 17, 1974. "Church speaker lauds pre-communist China," The Daily Press, January 8, 1979. "Tupper doctor returns to China," March 7, 1980. "With the spirit of adventure," The Weekender, March 15, 1980, cover and feature regarding Hyla Watters' 1980 trip to China. Coverage continues with, "Inside Part II of an interview with Dr. Hyla Watters," The Weekender, March 22, 1980. "B.P.W. Club honors Dr. Hyla Watters," Tupper Lake Free Press, October 31, 1984. "Dr. Watters Named Woman of the Year By Medical Society," n.d. "Dr. Hyla Watters Terminates Practice After Lifetime in Medical Service; Plans to Revisit Former Post in West Africa," n.d. "Florence Stuntz dies," n.d. "90 years young," n.d.

did.unittitle: Materials related to the death of Hyla S. Watters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Program of Service, United Community Church, 1987. Text to "Hyla Watters: God so Loves the Earth People," William J. Bostrom, August 8, 1987. Obituary for Hyla Stowell Watters. November 1989 letter from Betty Allen and Elsie Landstrom regarding a gift in honor of Hyla Watters to the Wuhu Hospital.

did.unittitle: Life membership in the CMA poster

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Calligraphy certificate of Hyla S. Watters' lifetime membership in the Chinese Medical Association (CMA).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Primarily botanical studnes and Liberian landscapes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tupper Lake lanscapes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Watercolors, including of Tupper Lake and Korea.

did.unittitle: En route to China, Nanking Language School, portraits

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs of Hyla and Ada Stowell Watters, general life in Wuhu, Charles and Anne Lindbergh's 1931 visit to Wuhu General Hospital.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including photographs of Hyla and Ada, Edward C. Perkins, Paul and Stella Sommerfreund, Max Birman, May Goldie, unnamed Wuhu patient. Family photograph of the Homer Stuntz family. Group photograph, "Commencement Day Wuhu General Hospital."

did.unittitle: Medical images from Wuhu General Hospital

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs related to the treatment of child patients at Wuhu General Hospital, showing eye drop applications, advanced bone sarcoma, and tongue cyst.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs of Chao Hsiu Chun (corresponding letter in box 1, folder 1), Liu Peng Cheng, Irene Chu Shih, Sun Po Fan, C.Y. Li, George C.C. Liang, the Dong family, Hsia The Ying, and others unnamed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs with Frances Culley, Wuhu patients, landscapes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs of the reopened hospital, UNRRA and Ichisan doctors, and a series of twenty film proofs potentially from UNRRA famine relief work and "feeding stations."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Twenty film proofs from an unknown location in China, depicting streetscapes, children, and rural landscapes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fourteen film proofs from China, depicting fishermen, children and caregivers, village life, and an empty church.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fifteen film proofs from China depicting crowded streetscapes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Thirty-one proofs depicting variously village life, groups of children, fishermen, a group trip, and a young family.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs with Mildred A. Black, Ganta Methodist Mission, and images from travling across Africa. Further information regarding the 1961 trip are in box 2, folder 1, which contains Mildred's journal from this time.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portraits of Hyla S. Watters at her Tupper Lake home, photographs with friends and family including Edgar Whitcomb, Wilfred Boayue, Hillary Stowell, Austin Stowell, and Dan Allen.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Image of a dog named Rube.

did.unittitle: Materials and Drafts Related to the Publication of Hyla Doc
Compiled excerpts from tapes and letters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1986 compilation described by Elsie Landstrom as, "letters and other documents telling the story of Hyla S. Watters, M.D. chief surgeon, Wuhu General Hospital, Wuhu, Anhwei, China, 1925-1948. A document prepared for her family, friends and archives."

did.unittitle: Front matter, family background, and chronological overview

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material from tapes, newspaper articles, the Stowell Genealogy, notes from Betty Stuntz Allen, and Hyla's account, "Growing-Up Days."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Smith College – Atlanta – Cornell Medical School – Bellevue Morristown, NJ – London School of Tropical Medicine – Preparations to leave for China." Transcribed letters 1917 – 1924, materials gathered from tapes and interviews.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Voyage to China – Nanking Language School – Work in Wuhu." Transcribed letters 1924-1925, extensive material from taped interviews.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Wuhu - Northern Expedition emergencies – New Hospital built – Korea – Letters from Ada Watters, Wuhu and India – Furlough." Gathered materials from taped interviews, transcribed 1927 letters from Korea, 1929 letters from Ada Stowell Watters in Lahore, 1929 letters from Ada and Hyla in Vienna.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Transcribed letters 1932-1935, material from taped interviews regarding 1927-1928, and the 1931 flood.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Return to China from furlough – Wuhu – Holiday in Japan – Return from furlough as Japanese taking Shanghai, Nanking, Wuhu." Transcribed letters 1930-1936, transcribed articles, materials from tapes including information on the 1931 visit of Charles and Anne Lindbergh to Wuhu General Hospital.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Elsie Landstrom's initial notes, photocopied newspaper articles on missionaries in China in 1939, transcribed stories and letters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Transcribed letters and taped interviews regarding the Japanese occupation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Transcribed letters, taped interviews, and articles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Taped interviews, photocopied articles.

did.unittitle: Miscellaneous material from Ganta, Liberia years

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Elsie Landstrom's initial notes, transcribed journal from the 1961 trip across Africa with Mildred Black.

did.unittitle: "1. Tell the Truth and Tie Square Knots"
"5. China was Far From Standing Still"
"6. There's No Putting into Words the Wonder of it All"
"7. Storm Beaten Craft of Every Draught"
"8. Disasters on a Grand Scale, and Good Things Too"
"10. I Never Thought I'd Be a Prisoner of War"
"11. Out Over The Edge of The World"
"12. Stand Not Upon the Order of Your Going"
"13. With Great Regret We Got That First Train"
"14. Witches, Witchdoctors, Devils and the Bush 1950-1961"
Edits and correspondence with Mildred Black
"14. Witches, Witchdoctors, Devils and the Bush 1950-1961," Mildred Black correction
Floppy disks containing the drafts of Hyla Doc

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Four floppy disks labeled "Hyla-Chaps 1-11," "Hyla-Chaps 12-end," "Hyla-summer 1988," "Hyla back up summer 1988."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One casette tape labeled, "1945 trip to Wuhu/Hu Li Jing, Black, Liberia.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three casette tapes. Interview of Hyla S. Watters conducted by Elsie Landstrom.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three cassette tapes captioned, "Interview with Hyla S. Watters, M.D. conducted at Tupper Lake, NY, September 28, 1983 by Lillian A. Johnson." Copyright 1983 by the Women's and World Divisions, the General Board of Global Missions (GBGM).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three casette tapes, conducted by Elsie Landstrom. Topics include, "internment," "repatriation," "back to W. China."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One casette tape, conducted by Elsie Landstrom. Topics including, "Kuling," "general board," James' tragic death."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tape of Hyla S. Watters' funeral service.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two casette tapes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Casette tape labeled one, including themes, "witch hunt," "Dr. Boayue."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Casette tape labeled two, including themes, "Dr. Boayue," "Egar Whitcomb," "Yao RN."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Casette tape labeled 3, including themes, "Ganta," "Leprosy village," "progressive creation."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Casette tape labeled 4, including themes, "Dr. Boayue to W.H.O.," leaving China 1949."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Casette tape labeled 5, including themes, "Jews in China," "Trip to Egypt."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Casette tape labeled 6, recording of a 1977 service.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Casette tape labeled 7, including themes, "return to Wuhu after Japanese," "Shanghai-are you a man or a woman?," "Wuhu during flood."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Casette tape labeled 8, including themes, "1945 return to China with U.S. army," "internment camp."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Casette labeled 9, including themes, "internment camp," "Korea," "India when Ada sick + died," "Liberia."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Casette labeled 10, including themes, "Christmas," "man who had head half cut off."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Casette tape labeled "Hyla Doc."

did.unittitle: Reviews and correspondence related to the publication of Hyla Doc
Correspondence and articles regarding the publication of Hyla Doc

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence from Elsie Landstrom. "Mish Kid," photocopied article regarding the publication of Hyla Doc, n.d. "Smith grad's life in China captured in book by friend," Daily Hampshire Gazette, February 6, 1991. "An Astounding Woman: Hyla S. Watters '15," article in The Sophian, Smith College, by Julia Hayden, September 24, 1992. "A woman surgeon's love affair with China," China Weekly, by H.R. Lan, March 7, 1991. Review Hyla Doc, Zion's Herald, n.d.

did.unittitle: Hyla Doc: Surgeon in China Through War and Revolution 1924-1949

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two copies of the published monograph Hyla Doc, edited by Elise H. Landstrom, Q.E.D. Press, Fort Bragg, California, 1991.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One copy of the published monograph Hyla Doc in Africa, edited by Elsie H. Landstrom, Q.E.D. Press, Fort Bragg, California, 1994.
